name.text: Finding the distance and displacement of a forest ranger as an application of vectors in real life

questiontext.text:
A forest ranger starts at a base station and travels \({@d1@}\) km due North to a lookout tower. From the tower, the ranger then travels \({@d2@}\) km due East to reach a remote campsite.

[[jsxgraph width="400px" height="400px"]] var board = JXG.JSXGraph.initBoard(divid, { boundingbox: [-2, 10, 10, -2], axis: true, showCopyright: false }); var n = {#d1#}; var e = {#d2#}; // Origin (Base Station) var p1 = board.create('point', [0, 0], {name: 'Base', fixed: true}); // Lookout Tower var p2 = board.create('point', [0, n], {name: 'Tower', color: 'blue'}); // Campsite var p3 = board.create('point', [e, n], {name: 'Camp', color: 'green'}); // Path segments (Vectors) board.create('arrow', [p1, p2], {strokeColor: 'blue', strokeWidth: 3}); board.create('arrow', [p2, p3], {strokeColor: 'green', strokeWidth: 3}); // Resultant Displacement board.create('arrow', [p1, p3], {strokeColor: 'red', strokeWidth: 2, dash: 2}); [[/jsxgraph]]

Using the knowledge of vectors, calculate:

  1. The total distance travelled by the ranger. [[input:ans1]] km [[validation:ans1]][[feedback:prt1]]
  2. The magnitude of the ranger's total displacement from the base station. [[input:ans2]] km [[validation:ans2]][[feedback:prt2]]

(Round your displacement to 2 decimal places.)

generalfeedback.text:
Worked Solution

To differentiate between scalar and vector quantities:

(a) Total Distance (Scalar Sum)

Distance only considers the path length regardless of direction:

\(\begin{align} \text{Distance} &= d_1 + d_2 \\ &= {@d1@} + {@d2@} \\ &= {@ta1@} \text{ km} \end{align}\)

(b) Displacement (Vector Magnitude)

Displacement is the straight-line distance from the start point to the end point. Since the movements are perpendicular (North and East), we use Pythagoras:

\(\begin{align} |\vec{s}| &= \sqrt{d_{\text{North}}^2 + d_{\text{East}}^2} \\ &= \sqrt{{@d1@}^2 + {@d2@}^2} \\ &= \sqrt{{@d1^2 + d2^2@}} \\ &= {@ta2@} \text{ km} \end{align}\)

prtcorrect.text: Correct answer, well done.

prtpartiallycorrect.text: Your answer is partially correct.

prtincorrect.text: Incorrect answer.
